obligations.reason: Visible RTL contains a JTAG/debug module with AXI master ports. Analysis should determine whether external JTAG can initiate arbitrary AXI transactions and whether those transactions are mediated by a debug authorization, lifecycle, privilege, or address-range guard.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/debug requester
obligations.object: SoC AXI address space and any protected registers/memory reachable through AXI
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable gating before JTAG-originated AXI read/write transactions are issued
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> adbg_top/adv_dbg_if -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> AXI master interface
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of debug selection/control registers
any top-level debug enable or test-mode inputs
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG/TAP requester signals and selected debug module
observed behavior: guard present or absent at issue point
path/reachability: TAP-to-AXI signal chain and top-level connections
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated and does not expose all top-level ports/connections; inspect RTL to confirm reachability and guards.

obligations.reason: The scope exposes a JTAG LINT debug path with address, write enable, data, byte-enable, and request outputs, plus a wrapper connected in pulp_soc. Check whether external debug requests are constrained before reaching memory or peripheral targets.
obligations.subject: external JTAG LINT debug requester
obligations.object: LINT/TCDM or local interconnect memory/register targets reachable from lint_add_o/lint_req_o
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization plus address-range/access policy restricting JTAG LINT requests to permitted debug regions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> lint_jtag_wrap/pulp_soc LINT/TCDM bus
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of debug control state
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior at adbg_lint_biu and lint_jtag_wrap
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to identify address map and whether lint_jtag_wrap or downstream interconnect applies policy.

obligations.reason: The TAP controls which debug module receives scan operations. A permission analysis should verify that instruction decode/module_select paths cannot enable protected bus access without an explicit debug authorization or lifecycle guard.
obligations.subject: external JTAG TAP controller
obligations.operation: select/enable debug module and route scan data
obligations.object: debug module selection controls and downstream bus-master capability
obligations.expected_guard: only authorized or lifecycle-enabled debug instructions should select bus-master/debug modules; unselected modules should not drive TDO or issue transactions
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_tap_top instruction/data register decode -> module_select_i/top_inhibit_o -> debug modules
state_conditions_to_check: TDO mux behavior for unselected modules
required_evidence_slots: subject: TAP instruction/register state controlled by TDI/TMS/TCK
operation: module selection and data routing
observed behavior in decode and top-level routing
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies TAP modules but not decode details; inspect defines and TAP FSM.

obligations.reason: OR1K debug modules and status register are present. Verify whether JTAG-originated operations affecting CPU state are conditioned on authorized debug mode and whether status/control bits are protected across reset and transitions.
obligations.subject: external JTAG OR1K debug requester
obligations.object: CPU debug registers, status/control state, execution halt/reset controls, or CPU-visible memory interface
obligations.expected_guard: CPU halt/debug authorization and privilege/lifecycle gating before reading/writing CPU registers or controlling execution
required_evidence_slots: operation: CPU register/status read/write or control
obligations.uncertainty: Top-level connectivity to a CPU/debug interface must be confirmed in current SoC configuration.

obligations.reason: Debug bus read data is externally shifted out via TDO. Check that read response capture and TDO muxing do not expose protected or stale data without the expected debug authorization and transaction validity conditions.
obligations.subject: external JTAG observer/requester
obligations.object: read data returned from SoC buses or CPU debug interface, including potentially secret/protected memory or registers
obligations.expected_guard: readback of protected data should require authorization and should not expose stale/unauthorized data after errors, reset, or deselection
obligations.candidate_path: debug read response path: AXI/LINT/OR1K bus data -> adbg_*_module shift register -> module_tdo_o/TDO
state_conditions_to_check: module deselection or aborted transaction residue
required_evidence_slots: operation: capture and shift read data
expected guard: auth plus valid response and module selection
observed behavior: read-data latch/TDO mux/reset clearing
obligations.uncertainty: Need code inspection for shift-register and data-latch behavior; protected target identity depends on downstream address map.

obligations.reason: Multiple debug modules have separate JTAG reset and system reset inputs. Check reset defaults and cross-domain transitions so that debug access is not implicitly authorized and stale response/status data is not retained for later TDO exposure.
obligations.subject: JTAG reset/system reset environment
obligations.operation: reset/transition into or out of debug-accessible state
obligations.object: debug enable/select state, transaction state machines, read-data/status registers
obligations.expected_guard: reset/debug mode transitions should not enable debug access by default or leave authorized/readable stale state after reset
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/debug reset inputs trstn_i and SoC reset/debug controls across adbg modules and top-level domains
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: safe reset default and explicit reauthorization
observed reset assignments and state retention
obligations.uncertainty: Authorization signals may be absent or named indirectly; inspect top-level integration for lifecycle/test controls.

obligations.reason: AXI ports expose AW/AR prot, user, region, id and LINT exposes aux/byte-enable signals. Verify whether these attributes are fixed or controllable and whether they could mark debug transactions as privileged/secure or bypass downstream access controls.
obligations.subject: JTAG-originated bus master
obligations.object: bus security/privilege attributes and downstream access-control interpretation
obligations.expected_guard: debug-originated transactions should carry restrictive security attributes or be range checked so they cannot be interpreted as privileged/secure accesses
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_axi_biu/adbg_lint_biu transaction formatting -> downstream bus attributes (prot/user/aux/be/size)
required_evidence_slots: operation: assign bus attributes and byte enables
expected guard: fixed non-secure/debug identity or access matrix enforcement
observed assignments and any JTAG-controlled fields
obligations.uncertainty: Downstream security meaning of user/aux/prot signals requires top-level/interconnect inspection.

obligations.reason: The SoC instantiates separate debug, JTAG, uDMA, FC, APB, ROM, and L2 bus objects. Check whether routing from JTAG/debug wrappers can reach protected memory/peripheral regions and whether requester identity or address-range controls are enforced at crossings.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug bus master entering SoC interconnect
obligations.operation: route JTAG/debug requests into SoC buses
obligations.object: SoC interconnect regions, APB debug/peripheral buses, L2/TCDM memory buses
obligations.expected_guard: wrappers/interconnect should preserve requester identity and enforce separation between JTAG debug, CPU, DMA, and peripheral access domains
obligations.candidate_path: lint_jtag_wrap and pulp_soc debug buses s_lint_debug_bus/s_lint_jtag_bus with APB debug/peripheral buses
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: interconnect access matrix/range check/identity preservation
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists bus instances but not crossbar implementations; inspect top-level connections and included defines.
